page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance based on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. This server features 64 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 64 vCPUs. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, and AES cryptography. The instance operates on a KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-threaded performance. Single-core execution is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25%, while single-threaded gzip compression is rated as poor. Conversely, multi-threaded capabilities are more robust. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression registers in the top 25%, and multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores reside in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks, along with Passmark CPU and memory scores, consistently align with average tier performance.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.c5.16xlarge is its low single-threaded performance contrasted against its high parallel processing capacity. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high density of dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, making it suitable for parallelizable workloads that do not require local storage or massive per-core memory allocations. Given these characteristics, the instance is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and moderate database operations, while being less optimal for single-threaded tasks or memory-intensive databases.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by a 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8163 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores (64 vCPUs) with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, 128.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it delivers a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between weak single-core execution and strong multi-threaded capabilities, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 25% and multi-core stress-ng performance in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth and database operations also perform at average levels. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for parallelized workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing, though it is less efficient for single-threaded applications or memory-heavy tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps bandwidth, delivering average multi-core and weak single-core performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized server featuring sixty-four dedicated physical cores and a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio for highly parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core
128.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage
Strong multi-threaded performance but weak single-core execution
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and parallel processing
